report_name: Australian Water Treatment Facilities Thwart Coordinated PLC Cyberattack

threat_actors.name: Unattributed Attackers
threat_actors.description: An unidentified threat actor or group that conducted a coordinated cyberattack against Australian water treatment facilities, targeting PLCs to manipulate chlorine levels.
threat_actors.goals: To override safety thresholds and manipulate the chemical distribution process, specifically for chlorine, to potentially cause a public health crisis.

attack_patterns.name: Manipulation of Control
attack_patterns.description: The attackers attempted to manipulate the chemical feed process to create a hazardous condition by altering chlorine distribution levels.

attack_patterns.name: Modify Parameter
attack_patterns.description: The attackers attempted to modify the setpoints within the PLCs to alter the amount of chlorine distributed into the water supply.

identities.name: Australian municipal water treatment facilities
identities.description: A class of critical infrastructure organizations in Australia whose chemical feed PLCs were targeted in a coordinated cyberattack.
